steps.title: Notice the repeated action
steps.content: The passage uses habitual time markers such as "Every few days" and "would" to describe Abuela Evila washing Élida's hair. The scene is presented as a recurring memory from Grande's childhood.

steps.title: Reject unsupported purposes
steps.content: A focuses on sibling interaction, C invents relationship growth over time, and D shifts to feelings about a place. The remaining choice captures the passage's purpose: describing a regular childhood occurrence. The answer is B.
